channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: What to know about the Canvas hack of student data
item.description: Millions of students in universities and K-12 districts had their data compromised this week as a hack took down Canvas, a classroom management tool used all over the country.
item.content:encoded: Millions of students in universities and K-12 districts had their data compromised this week as a hack took down Canvas, a classroom management tool used all over the country.
item.dc:creator: Sequoia Carrillo

item.title: Bobby Cox, Hall of Fame manager of Atlanta Braves, dies at age 84
item.description: The Braves announced Cox's death on Saturday. He managed the team to prominence during the 1990s and the team's only championship in 1995, before retiring after the 2010 season.
item.content:encoded:
The Braves announced Cox's death on Saturday. He managed the team to prominence during the 1990s and the team's only championship in 1995, before retiring after the 2010 season.

(Image credit: John Bazemore)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: CDC says threat of widespread outbreak of hantavirus remains low
item.description: As hantavirus has dominated headlines, sparking fears of another debilitating pandemic, the CDC stressed that the risk of sweeping contagion was small.
item.content:encoded:
As hantavirus has dominated headlines, sparking fears of another debilitating pandemic, the CDC stressed that the risk of sweeping contagion was small.

(Image credit: Chris McGrath)
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise

item.title: Frontier Airlines plane strikes and kills pedestrian
item.description: Denver International Airport said the person had jumped a fence and dashed into the aircraft's path minutes before being struck.
item.content:encoded:
Denver International Airport said the person had jumped a fence and dashed into the aircraft's path minutes before being struck.

(Image credit: David Zalubowski)
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise

item.title: Florida's controversial 'Alligator Alcatraz' immigration detention center faces closure
item.description: Florida's controversial immigration detention center nicknamed "Alligator Alcatraz" may be closing soon. It's been very expensive to operate.
item.content:encoded: Florida's controversial immigration detention center nicknamed "Alligator Alcatraz" may be closing soon. It's been very expensive to operate.
item.dc:creator: Meghan Bowman

item.title: How Arizona could benefit from a proposal to stabilize the Colorado River
item.description: Arizona farmers would be among the first affected by a new proposal to reduce water delivers from the Colorado River. They hope it leads to longer term stability.
item.content:encoded: Arizona farmers would be among the first affected by a new proposal to reduce water delivers from the Colorado River. They hope it leads to longer term stability.
item.dc:creator: Katya Mendoza

item.title: Southern Republicans redistrict after Supreme Court rules, Dems lose big in Virginia
item.description: The Supreme Court weakened minority voting rights and prompted Republicans in four states to move to redistrict as part of Trump's push. A court nullified Democratic redistricting in Virginia.
item.content:encoded: The Supreme Court weakened minority voting rights and prompted Republicans in four states to move to redistrict as part of Trump's push. A court nullified Democratic redistricting in Virginia.
item.dc:creator: Larry Kaplow

item.title: Soccer was once considered niche in the U.S. Then came the 1994 World Cup
item.description: Soccer — or football as it's known around the globe — was far from mainstream in the U.S. leading up to the 1994 World Cup. But in the end, the tournament was considered a resounding success. How exactly did that happen?
item.content:encoded:
Soccer — or football as it's known around the globe — was far from mainstream in the U.S. leading up to the 1994 World Cup. But in the end, the tournament was considered a resounding success. How exactly did that happen?

(Image credit: Lois Bernstein)
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: Why Trump's efforts to force Iran to concede to U.S. demands aren't working
item.description: The Virginia Supreme Court has delivered a striking blow to Democrats in the redistricting battle they're waging against Republicans.
item.content:encoded: The Virginia Supreme Court has delivered a striking blow to Democrats in the redistricting battle they're waging against Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Northam

item.title: A young mom at her wits' end tossed out her dirty dishes. Then they turned up clean
item.description: A young mother felt overwhelmed raising her three kids alone. Unable to face a sink full of dirty dishes, she threw them out. A neighbor returned them, washed and shiny, along with a message of hope.
item.content:encoded: A young mother felt overwhelmed raising her three kids alone. Unable to face a sink full of dirty dishes, she threw them out. A neighbor returned them, washed and shiny, along with a message of hope.
item.dc:creator: Jordan-Marie Smith
